2603.15863.title: Interpretative Interfaces: Designing for AI-Mediated Reading Practices and the Knowledge Commons
2603.15863.paper_summary: Explainable AI (XAI) interfaces seek to make large language models more transparent, yet explanation alone does not produce understanding. Explaining a system's behavior is not the same as being able to engage with it, to probe and interpret its operations through direct manipulation. This distinction matters for scientific disciplines in particular: scientists who increasingly rely on LLMs for reading, citing, and producing literature reviews have little means of directly engaging with how these models process and transform the texts they generate. In this ongoing design research project, I argue for a shift from explainability to interpretative engagement. This shift moves away from accounts of system behavior to instead enable users to manipulate a model's intermediate representations. Drawing on textual scholarship, computational poetics, and the history of reading and writing technologies, including practices such as marginalia, glosses, indices, and annotation systems, I propose interpretative interfaces as interactive environments in which non-expert users can intervene in the representational space of a language model. More specifically, such interfaces will allow users to select a token and follow its trajectory through the model's intermediate layers. This way, they can observe how its semantic position shifts as context is processed, and possibly annotate the transformations they find useful or meaningful. The same way readers can create their own maps within a book through annotations and bookmarks, interpretative interfaces will allow users to inscribe their reading of a model's internal representations. The goal of this project is to reframe AI interpretability as an interaction design project rather than a purely technical one, and to open a path toward AI-mediated reading that supports interpretative engagement and critical stewardship of scientific knowledge.
2603.15863.paper_summary_zh: 可解釋的人工智慧（XAI）介面旨在使大型語言模型更加透明，然而僅僅提供解釋並不能產生理解。解釋系統的行為並不等同於能夠與其互動，通過直接操作來探查和解釋其運作。這一區別對科學學科尤其重要：科學家們越來越依賴大型語言模型來閱讀、引用和撰寫文獻綜述，但卻幾乎沒有直接參與這些模型如何處理和轉換生成文本的手段。在這個持續進行的設計研究項目中，我主張從可解釋性轉向詮釋性參與。這一轉變不再關注系統行為的描述，而是使使用者能夠操作模型的中介表示。基於文本學、計算詩學以及閱讀和寫作技術的歷史，包括邊註、註釋、索引和註解系統等實踐，我提出詮釋性介面作為互動環境，使非專家使用者能夠介入語言模型的表現空間。更具體地說，這些介面將允許使用者選擇一個標記並跟踪其在模型中介層的軌跡。這樣，他們可以觀察到隨著上下文的處理，其語義位置如何變化，並可能對他們認為有用或有意義的轉變進行註解。就像讀者可以通過註解和書籤在書中創建自己的地圖一樣，詮釋性介面將允許使用者記錄他們對模型內部表示的理解。這個項目的目標是將人工智慧的可解釋性重新框架為一個互動設計項目，而不是純粹的技術項目，並為人工智慧介導的閱讀開辟一條支持詮釋性參與和科學知識的批判性管理的道路。
2603.15863.author: Gabrielle Benabdallah et.al.
